rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the issue to the first invalid identifier or helper on the same line and fix the root cause by ensuring all references are valid and types match.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope and that expression types match expected scalar types (int, real, bool).
Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scopes.
Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Left-hand side of an assign statement must be an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare types explicitly using int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assumptions and assignments there.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL component scope.
